title: Biometric Authentication Using Chest Wall Displacement Recorded by VHF-Band Loop Antenna

method.input_representation: Non-fiducial features: Autocorrelation (AC), Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT), and Mel-frequency cepstrum coefficient (MFCC)
method.training_strategy: Three-fold cross-validation for authentication; SMOTE applied to handle class imbalance.

evaluation.context: Best result achieved in resting state (intra-state) using SVM with combined AC/DCT and MFCC features
Activity recognition models (except SVM) achieved AUC > 0.950; user authentication SVM model categorized as excellent (0.900 < AUC < 1.000)

evaluation_protocol: Three-fold cross-validation for user authentication; five-fold cross-validation for activity recognition.

key_contribution: Proposed a non-contact biometric authentication system using chest wall displacement via a wearable VHF-band loop antenna
Integrated activity recognition to dynamically select the optimal authentication model based on user state (resting vs. working)
main_strength: Eliminates the need for skin-contact electrodes (reducing physical restriction)
Immune to light source interference compared to PPG
main_weakness: Testing restricted to semi-controlled settings; lack of evaluation during high-mobility activities like walking
insights.notes_for_review: The study demonstrates feasibility but explicitly notes that future work is required for liveness detection and presentation attack (PA) countermeasures.
